metadata.data_source: China Food Composition Table (6th Ed.) + USDA FoodData Central

foods.name: Oats
foods.notes: Rich in beta-glucan, helps lower cholesterol

foods.name: Brown Rice
foods.notes: Retains bran layer, richer in nutrients

foods.name: White Rice
foods.notes: Refined and processed, significant nutrient loss

foods.name: Whole Wheat Bread
foods.notes: Made with 100% whole wheat flour

foods.name: Egg
foods.notes: Complete protein source, contains all essential amino acids

foods.name: Chicken Breast
foods.notes: Excellent low-fat protein source

foods.name: Salmon
foods.notes: Wild salmon has higher Omega-3 content

foods.name: Tofu
foods.notes: Contains soy isoflavones, beneficial for women's health

foods.name: Milk
foods.notes: Excellent source of calcium and protein; use with caution for lactose intolerance

foods.name: Yogurt
foods.notes: Contains probiotics, promotes gut health

foods.name: Spinach
foods.notes: Contains oxalic acid which affects calcium absorption; blanching recommended

foods.name: Broccoli
foods.notes: Contains sulforaphane, a potent antioxidant

foods.name: Carrot
foods.notes: Rich in beta-carotene, beneficial for eye health

foods.name: Tomato
foods.notes: Rich in lycopene, a potent antioxidant

foods.name: Apple
foods.notes: Rich in pectin, helps lower cholesterol

foods.name: Banana
foods.notes: Contains tryptophan, aids sleep

foods.name: Orange
foods.notes: Whole fruit is healthier than juice

foods.name: Strawberry
foods.notes: One of the fruits with the strongest antioxidant capacity

foods.name: Almonds
foods.notes: Rich in unsaturated fatty acids, beneficial for heart health

foods.name: Walnuts
foods.notes: One of the best plant-based Omega-3 sources

foods.name: Sweet Potato
foods.notes: More nutritious than white sweet potato, relatively lower GI

foods.name: Potato
foods.notes: Skin is highly nutritious; recommended to eat with skin

foods.name: Corn
foods.notes: Contains lutein, beneficial for eye health

foods.name: Beef
foods.notes: Choose lean cuts for better health

foods.name: Pork
foods.notes: Rich in thiamine (Vitamin B1)

foods.name: Shrimp
foods.notes: Contains astaxanthin, a potent antioxidant

foods.name: Chinese Cabbage
foods.notes: Main vegetable in northern China winters

foods.name: Cucumber
foods.notes: Water content up to 95%

foods.name: Green Bell Pepper
foods.notes: One of the vegetables with the highest Vitamin C content

foods.name: Eggplant
foods.notes: Skin contains anthocyanins, a potent antioxidant

foods.name: Lettuce
foods.notes: Common Salad Vegetable

foods.name: Pear
foods.notes: High in soluble fiber

foods.name: Blueberry
foods.notes: One of the fruits with the strongest antioxidant capacity

foods.name: Kiwi
foods.notes: Contains actinidin enzyme, aids protein digestion

foods.name: Mango
foods.notes: Known as the king of fruits, nutritionally rich

foods.name: Watermelon
foods.notes: Water content up to 92%

foods.name: Grape
foods.notes: Grape skin contains abundant antioxidants

foods.name: Peanuts
foods.notes: Actually a legume, not a nut

foods.name: Chia Seeds
foods.notes: Best plant-based Omega-3 source

foods.name: Olive Oil
foods.notes: Core fat of the Mediterranean diet

foods.name: Salmon Oil
foods.notes: Excellent Omega-3 supplement source

foods.name: Dark Chocolate
foods.notes: Choose 70%+ cocoa content

foods.name: Green Tea
foods.notes: Contains EGCG, a potent antioxidant

foods.name: Black Coffee
foods.notes: Drink in moderation, avoid excess

foods.name: Low-fat Yogurt
foods.notes: Choose unsweetened or low-sugar version

foods.name: Quinoa
foods.notes: One of the few plant-based complete protein sources

foods.name: Adzuki Bean
foods.notes: TCM considers adzuki beans to have diuretic and detoxifying properties

foods.name: Lentils
foods.notes: Extremely high in folate

foods.name: Cooked Brown Rice
foods.notes: Brown rice: water = 1:2.5 to cook

foods.name: Chickpeas
foods.notes: Main ingredient in hummus
